News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Trump Ultimatum Looms, As Israel Warns Of 'Weeks' Of More Fighting Against Iran, Hezbollah RFE/RL 23 Mar 2026 -- As US President Donald Trump's devastating deadline looms for Iran to open the crucial Strait of Hormuz, the Israeli military warned its citizens that "more weeks" remain in the twin wars against Iran and Hezbollah.

  • USS Gerald R. Ford Arrives in Souda Bay US Navy 23 Mar 2026 -- The USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) arrived at Naval Support Activity Souda Bay, March 23, 2026, for maintenance and repairs after operating in the Red Sea.

  • 'Until final victory': IRGC unleashes 77th wave of missiles, drones at enemy targets Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) carried out the 77th wave of Operation True Promise 4 against Israeli and American targets late on Monday, deploying precision-guided Kheibar Shekan missile systems and solid-fuel Zolfaqar missiles as well as explosive-laden drones.
  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 23 Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- On Monday, March 23, 2026, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Army conducted multiple operations as part of Operation True Promise 4, which was launched immediately after the US-Israeli coalition carried out an unprovoked act of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran on February 28.
  • 'We negotiate with enemies with impact-driven strikes,' IRGC says as it announces 78th wave of reprisal Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) announces staging the 78th wave of its underway retaliatory Operation True Promise 4, targeting such highly sensitive Israeli targets as Dimona, Tel Aviv, and Eilat as well as several US military bases in the region.
  • Iran's air defenses shoot down US drone over Persian Gulf coast Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Iran's army air defense units intercepted and shot down a US unmanned aerial vehicle as it flew over the Persian Gulf coastline.
  • Iran's Army strikes Israeli, US air bases housing advanced fighter jets Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Republic of Iran Army has launched retaliatory drone strikes on Israeli and US air bases in the region, where their fighter jets are stationed.
  • IRGC targets US Fifth Naval Fleet, other sites in 76th wave of reprisal attacks Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched a fresh wave of retaliatory airstrikes, targeting military infrastructure belonging to the hostile US and Israeli regimes.
  • Iranian Navy downs two more US drones, bringing total number to 129 Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian Navy says its defensive and anti-aircraft systems have successfully detected and destroyed two drones belonging to the terrorist army of the United States.
  • IRGC warns US: Any threat will be met with proportional, deterring response Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has issued a stern warning to the United States, emphasizing that any threat to Iran would be met with a proportional and deterring response.
  • Iran to Mine Sea Lanes in Persian Gulf in Case of Attack - Defense Council Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- Tehran will mine all sea lanes in the Persian Gulf in the event of an attack on the coast of the country or islands, the Iranian Defense Council said on Monday.
  • Passage through Strait of Hormuz possible only through coordination with Iran: Defense Council Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Defense Council says the passage of non-hostile vessels through the Strait of Hormuz is possible only via coordination with Iranian officials, amid ongoing US-Israeli aggression against Iran.
  • IRGC strikes Israeli military sites and US Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has successfully executed the 75th wave of its ongoing retaliatory Operation True Promise 4, codenamed "O Fatima Al-Zahra (SA)," in honor of martyred Iranian commanders and as a firm response to continued Zionist and American aggression.

  • Trump Sees 'Very Serious Chance' Of Iran Deal As He Delays Strikes On Energy Targets RFE/RL 23 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said he sees a "very serious chance" of making a deal with Tehran after he revealed the two sides held talks over the weekend that led him to delay any strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure for five days.
  • Trump backed down after Iran threatened to retaliate, no talks underway: Report Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has backed away from his 48-hour ultimatum to strike Iran's power plants after the Islamic Republic warned that all energy and power installations in the region would be targeted in retaliation, a report said.
  • US-Iran Talks Occurred on Sunday Night, Involved Kushner, Witkoff - Reports Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- The latest round of talks between the United States and Iran happened on the night of March 22 and involved US special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushener, Fox News reported on Monday, citing President Donald Trump.
  • Trump Orders Five-Day Pause on Iran Power Plant Strikes Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- The United States and Iran had very positive and productive talks over the past two days, US President Donald Trump said on Monday.
  • Ranking Member Shaheen Presses DFC CEO for Answers on Risks to Taxpayer and Potential Benefits to China from $20 Billion Strait of Hormuz Maritime Insurance Proposal US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 23 Mar 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, sent a letter to the CEO of the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC), Benjamin Black, raising serious concerns about the Administration's reported plan to provide up to $20 billion in taxpayer-funded political risk reinsurance for maritime trade through the Strait of Hormuz. Senator Shaheen underscored the need for greater transparency and oversight, warning that the proposal could expose U.S. taxpayers to significant financial risk while potentially benefiting strategic competitors like China.
  • Between buildup and bottleneck -- Washington's narrowing path in war with Iran Global Times 23 Mar 2026 -- The United States is deploying additional troops and warships to the Middle East even as its president, Donald Trump, suggested a possible "winding down" of strikes against Iran.

  • You have no right to interfere in Persian Gulf security: Iran top military command to extra-regional states Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Iran's highest operational command unit warns extra-regional states against trying to interfere in the Persian Gulf region's security, asserting that the Islamic Republic is perfectly capable of securing the region in cooperation with friendly regional nations.
  • Iran rejects Trump's claim, confirms no talks held with US amid ongoing war Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The spokesman for Iran's Foreign Ministry has dismissed US President Donald Trump's claims of talks with Iran, saying Tehran has had no negotiations with Washington over the past 24 days of the unprovoked US-Israel war against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran urges Russia, China to block US abuse of UN Security Council Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi urges Russia and China to prevent the United States from abusing the United Nations Security Council to commit more crimes against Iran and exacerbate tensions in the region.
  • Iran regrets abuse of regional states' soil for US-Israeli attacks Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says it is regrettable that the United States and the Israeli regime continue abusing the territory of Muslim countries in the region to carry out attacks on the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran urges IAEA to slam US threats to target Bushehr nuclear power plant Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Permanent Mission to International Organizations in Vienna urges the UN nuclear watchdog to "swiftly" denounce US threats to attack a nuclear power plant in the south of the country.
  • Tehran: World grown thoroughly exhausted with US-Israeli 'false flag storylines' Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman says the world has grown thoroughly exhausted with the US-Israeli "false flag storylines."
  • Iran Not Negotiating With US, Fake News Used to Manipulate Markets - Parliament Speaker Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- Iran has not held talks with the United States, and fake news is being used to manipulate markets, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on Monday.
  • Tehran Not Holding Talks With Washington - Iranian Foreign Ministry Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- Tehran has not held direct talks with Washington but has received messages from the United States via mediators regarding its desire to begin negotiations to end the war, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said on Monday

  • Statement by PM Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 23 Mar 2026
  • Barak blasts Netanyahu: 'Stop lying - you can't destroy Iran's nuclear, missile capabilities' Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Former Israeli prime minister Ehud Barak on Monday launched a blistering attack at the regime's incumbent political and military leadership, slamming them for peddling "blatant" lies over the war against Iran and noting that the regime has no strategy to end the war.

  • Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis UN News 23 Mar 2026 -- Since the start of the Middle East conflict with Israeli and US strikes on Iran on 28 February, concerns have been growing over rising oil and commodity prices.
  • Remarks by UNICEF Deputy Executive Director, Ted Chaiban, at the UN Secretary General Spokesperson's noon briefing: Across the Middle East, a generation of children is being pulled deeper into crisis UNICEF 23 Mar 2026
  • War in the Middle East: Cultural treasures damaged, with more at risk UN News 23 Mar 2026 -- Since the outbreak of war on 28 February, several unique sites of cultural significance have been damaged in Iran, Israel and Lebanon, alongside immense suffering, displacement and death.

  • IEA: Global economy faces 'major, major threat' because of Iran war Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 23 Mar 2026 -- War in the Middle East has 'severely' damaged more than 40 energy assets across nine countries, said the head of the International Energy Agency, as he warned of deepening risks to the global economy.

  • China Urges De-Escalation As Hormuz Crisis Deepens RFE/RL 23 Mar 2026 -- China has warned over an "uncontrollable situation" amid the escalating Strait of Hormuz crisis and urged all sides to halt military operations as the war involving Iran enters a critical phase and threatens global energy supplies.

  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 Mar 2026
  • Lavrov, Araghchi Discuss Situation in Persian Gulf Zone by Phone- Russian Foreign Ministry Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi have discussed the situation in the Persian Gulf zone during a phone conversation, the Russian Foreign Ministry said on Monday.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 23 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 23 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 23 Mar 2026

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 23 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 23 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Drone Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 23 Mar 2026
  • HRH the Crown Prince, French President Discuss Regional Escalation; Macron Reiterates Solidarity with Saudi Arabia SPA 23 Mar 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received a phone call from French President Emmanuel Macron, during which they reviewed the latest regional developments and their repercussions

  • Don't put our energy infrastructure in danger, Persian Gulf states warn Trump Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Arab officials from Persian Gulf littoral states have reportedly called on US President Donald Trump to step back from threats against Iran's critical infrastructure, warning that Tehran's retaliation would be swift and severe.
  • Azerbaijani, Iranian FMs hold phone talk Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 23 Mar 2026 -- On March 23, Jeyhun Bayramov, Azerbaijan's Minister of Foreign Affairs, held a telephone conversation with Seyed Abbas Araghchi, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran.
  • Libya's grand mufti urges Muslims to support Iran, highlights concept of 'no neutrality' in Qur'an Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Libya's Grand Mufti Sheikh Sadiq al-Ghariani has called upon Muslims worldwide to support Iran amid the US-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic, warning that deployment of US troops in the Arab world would destabilize the region.
  • Summoning of the Iranian Ambassador to the United Kingdom: FCDO statement UK FCDO 23 Mar 2026 -- The Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office has today summoned the Ambassador of the Islamic Republic of Iran to the United Kingdom in response to two individuals being charged on suspicion of providing assistance to a foreign intelligence service.
  • Readout: COBR(M) - Iran economic and domestic impacts: 23 March 2026 UK FCDO 23 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister chaired a COBR(M) meeting today, focussing on the domestic economic impact of the ongoing situation in the Middle East.
  • Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi addresses the Lok Sabha on the ongoing conflict in West Asia India PIB 23 Mar 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi today addressed the Lok Sabha on the ongoing conflict in West Asia and the wide-ranging challenges it has posed for India
  • English rendering of PM's remarks in the Lok Sabha on the ongoing conflict in West Asia India PIB 23 Mar 2026

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Statement by Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell on Implementation of Revised Media In-Brief DoD 23 Mar 2026
  • US, allied forces strengthen Arctic readiness during exercise Cold Response 26 US Air Force 23 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Air Force Airmen, alongside Norwegian and allied partners, successfully concluded participation in exercise Cold Response 26, a Norwegian-led multinational training event designed to enhance collective defense, sharpen warfighting skills and reinforce deterrence capabilities in the high north.
  • U.S. 4th Fleet Announces Southern Seas 2026 Deployment US Navy 23 Mar 2026 -- Nimitz-class aircraft carrier USS Nimitz (CVN 68) will deploy to the U.S. Southern Command area of responsibility as part of U.S. Naval Forces Southern Command/U.S. 4th Fleet's Southern Seas 2026 deployment.
  • U.S. 7th Fleet flagship, Philippine forces conduct Maritime Cooperative Activity US Navy 23 Mar 2026 -- The combined forces of the Philippines and the United States conducted a Maritime Cooperative Activity (MCA) within the Philippine Exclusive Economic Zone, March 20, 2026.
  • Future USS Harvey C. Barnum Jr. (DDG 124) Arrives at Naval Station Norfolk Homeport Ahead of Commissioning US Navy 23 Mar 2026 -- The newest Arleigh Burke class guided-missile destroyer, the future USS Harvey C. Barnum Jr. (DDG 124), arrived at its homeport, Naval Station Norfolk, on March 20. The arrival precedes the warship's commissioning ceremony, which is scheduled for April 11, 2026.
  • USS Mustin returns to forward-deployed Naval forces in Yokosuka US Navy 23 Mar 2026 -- Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Mustin (DDG 89) arrived at Commander, Fleet Activities Yokosuka (CFAY) March 23, 2026, marking its return to U.S. 7th Fleet after nearly five years in San Diego. The ship was previously forward-deployed to Yokosuka from 2006 to 2021.

  • Partnering to Strengthen Resilience - NATO's Military Strategic Partnerships Conference 2026 kicks off in Sarajevo NATO SHAPE 23 Mar 2026 -- The annual Military Strategic Partnerships Conference (MSPC), hosted by Supreme Headquarters Allied Powers Europe (SHAPE) Partnerships Directorate, is being held in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina from Mar. 23 to 25, 2026.
  • Poland marks 20 years of F-16 service as jets bolster NATO Airpower NATO 23 Mar 2026 -- RAMSTEIN, Germany -- Poland marked two decades of F-16 operations that have strengthened NATO Airpower, sharpening the Alliance's ability to deter and defend every inch on NATO territory, especially on its eastern flank.

  • Defence Forces of Ukraine receive new high-speed JEDI Shahed Hunter interceptor drones to counter Shahed-type threats Ukraine MOD 23 Mar 2026
  • Joint statement from Tallinn Mechanism member states: 10 March 2026 UK FCDO 23 Mar 2026 -- Tallinn Mechanism members gave a joint statement in London on 10 March 2026, reaffirming support for Ukraine's civilian cyber defence.

  • 28th anniversary of J-10's maiden flight witnesses system integration, AEW coordination Global Times 23 Mar 2026 -- As Monday marked the 28th anniversary of the maiden flight of Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Air Force's J-10 fighter jet, CCTV News reported on Monday that multiple units have in recent days conducted high-intensity drills with J-10C fighter jets under complex electromagnetic conditions, including confrontational combat, close-range dogfights, and penetration strike training.
  • 40th China-Vietnam joint patrol in Beibu Gulf concluded PRC MND 23 Mar 2026 -- The 40th joint patrol in the Beibu Gulf and the first joint maritime training between China and Vietnam successfully concluded on March 20. The two-day joint patrol involved the Chinese naval frigates Hengyang (Hull 568) and Enshi (Hull 627), and the Vietnamese naval frigates Tran Hung Dao (Hull 015) and Ly Thai To (Hull 012).

  • Defense minister says Cabinet bill more comprehensive than opposition CNA 23 Mar 2026 -- Defense Minister Wellington Koo (顧立雄) said Monday that a government-proposed NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.6 billion) special defense budget is the most comprehensive among competing proposals, as it supports long-term domestic weapons production.
  • First US-made F-16V delivery in September 'feasible': Defense minister CNA 23 Mar 2026 -- Defense Minister Wellington Koo (顧立雄) said Monday that a September delivery date for the first of 66 F-16V fighter jets Taiwan purchased from the United States is "feasible."

  • Turning ocean data into decision superiority Australia DOD 23 Mar 2026 -- For more than two decades, a quiet but powerful platform has been working behind the scenes to support Navy missions across the globe.

  • Saudi Arabia Offers Condolences to Qatar and Türkiye over Helicopter Crash SPA 23 Mar 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's sincere condolences and sympathy to the families of the martyrs of duty and to the governments and peoples of the State of Qatar and the Republic of Türkiye, following the crash of a helicopter due to a technical malfunction while conducting a routine mission in Qatari territorial waters, which resulted in the death of the helicopter crew from the Qatari Armed Forces and the Qatari-Turkish joint forces

  • EDA briefs EU's Committee of the Regions for defence readiness European Defence Agency 23 Mar 2026

  • The increase of Lithuania's defence spending is a success story we should share, says Vice Minister of National Defence K. Aleksa Lithuania MND 23 Mar 2026
  • The German Brigade in Lithuania is a signal that Alliance defence begins here and now, says Minister R. Kaunas Lithuania MND 23 Mar 2026

  • The Romanian military personnel deployed to Iraq returned to the country Romania - Ministry of National Defence 23 Mar 2026 -- All 117 Romanian soldiers deployed to the Iraqi theater of operations returned to Romania on Saturday, March 21, following the decision to adjust NATO's posture within NATO Mission Iraq.

  • PH Navy's hull renumbering reflects growing capabilities Philippine News Agency 23 Mar 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) has renumbered the hull or pennant numbers of its ships in line with its ongoing acquisition of more modern surface combatants aimed at boosting its fleet's capabilities.
  • PH, France defense execs to tackle growing security ties in Paris meet Philippine News Agency 23 Mar 2026 -- The Philippines and France are expected to deepen their security ties as ranking defense leaders of the two nations will meet on the sidelines of the 2026 Paris Defence and Strategy Forum (PDSF) this week.

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Kenyan President Ruto US Dept. of State 23 Mar 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Canadian Minister of Foreign Affairs Anand US Dept. of State 23 Mar 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 23 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 23 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • As Hezbollah Steps Up Attacks, Who Really Calls The Shots? RFE/RL 23 Mar 2026 -- When rocket fire from Lebanon hit the northern Israeli town of Kiryat Shmona, it damaged a bus and left a man in his 50s with a serious shrapnel wound to his face, according to emergency services.

  • IDF Says General Staff Approved Plans for Ground Operations in Lebanon Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- Israel Defense Forces (IDF) Chief of the General Staff Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir has approved plans for ground operations against the Hezbollah movement in Lebanon, the IDF said.

  • Hezbollah strikes force Israeli defensive shift, exposing southern skies: Intel assessment Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The latest intelligence assessment of the conflict zone in northern occupied territories reveals that Hezbollah's sustained military operations have compelled the Israeli regime to reconfigure its air defense posture, effectively stripping the southern occupied territories of aerial protection.
  • Hezbollah says 'preparing for long war with Israel, has many 'surprises' in store Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The head of Hezbollah's Liaison and Coordination Unit, Wafiq Safa, says the Lebanese resistance is ready for a long war with the Israeli regime and has many surprises in store.
  • Hezbollah conducts 63 military operations against Israel in 24 hours Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- The Lebanese Islamic resistance movement Hezbollah has announced that it carried out 63 military operations targeting positions and centers of the Israeli regime's occupation army over the past 24 hours.

  • UN expert warns torture has become 'state doctrine' in Israel, making prisons instruments of genocide and torture OHCHR 23 Mar 2026 -- Israel's systematic torture of Palestinians, long shielded by decades of impunity and political cover, has become a defining instrument of ongoing genocide in the occupied Palestinian territory, a UN expert warned today.

  • Indonesia rejects $1 billion fee for Gaza 'Board of Peace' amid domestic criticism Press TV 23 Mar 2026 -- Indonesia has, amid domestic criticism, rejected making any financial commitment to join a United States-backed Gaza initiative as a permanent member.
  • SEK 555 million in new humanitarian aid for Gaza Government Offices of Sweden 23 Mar 2026
  • Spanish Government condemns Israeli statements regarding destruction of civilian infrastructure in Lebanon Spain MFA 23 Mar 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Ukraine, Sudan UNOCHA 23 Mar 2026

    • China affirms sovereignty over Xisha Islands, rejects Vietnam's remarks on China's land reclamation activities on islands: Chinese FM Global Times 23 Mar 2026 -- When asked to comment on Vietnamese officials' remarks over the weekend on China's land reclamation activities in the Xisha Islands, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Monday that the Xisha Islands are China's inherent territory and there is no dispute over them. China's necessary construction on its own territory is aimed at improving the living conditions of island residents and serving local economic development, Lin said.
  • Statement of the DFA Spokesperson on Maritime Affairs Philippines DFA 23 Mar 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs addresses recent public commentary regarding discussions on a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) and the China Coast Guard (CCG).
  • DFA assures no sensitive ops in PH-China coast guards MOU Philippine News Agency 23 Mar 2026 -- The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) on Monday clarified that ongoing discussions to amend a memorandum of understanding (MOU) between the Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) and the China Coast Guard (CCG) are limited in scope and do not involve sensitive operations.

  • Vietnam protests China's development of disputed reef in South China Sea Radio Free Asia 23 Mar 2026 -- Vietnam has condemned China's land reclamation activities at Antelope reef in the disputed Paracel Islands, following reports of accelerated dredging, landfill and construction operations there that, according to a U.S.-based think tank, could make it "China's largest feature" in the South China Sea.

News Reports

  • White House border czar says ICE agents will assist at airport security points amid DHS shutdown People's Daily 23 Mar 2026 -- White House border czar Tom Homan said Sunday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents will assist at busy airport security checkpoints amid a Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown that has lasted more than a month.
  • U.S. Senate advances DHS secretary nomination in procedural vote People's Daily 23 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. Senate on Sunday advanced Senator Markwayne Mullin's nomination for Department of Homeland Security (DHS) secretary, just over two weeks after President Donald Trump announced that he will replace current DHS Secretary Kristi Noem.

  • Russian Citizen Sentenced to Prison for Hacking into U.S. Companies and Enabling Major Cybercrime Groups to Extort Tens of Millions of Dollars US Dept. of Justice 23 Mar 2026 -- A court in the Southern District of Indiana today sentenced a Russian citizen, Aleksei Volkov, to 81 months in prison for assisting major cybercrime groups, including the Yanluowang ransomware group, in carrying out numerous attacks against U.S. companies and other organizations.

  • Hungary's Orban Orders Investigation Into Wiretapping of Hungarian Foreign Minister Sputnik 23 Mar 2026 -- Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban on Monday ordered the justice ministry to investigate the wiretapping of Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto, describing it as an attack on the country.

  • SSU exposes Hungarian intelligence officer who run Budapest's spy network in Zakarpattia in 2025 Security Service of Ukraine 23 Mar 2026 -- The Counterintelligence Department of the Security Service of Ukraine has identified a Hungarian military intelligence officer who led an agent network exposed by the SSU in the spring of 2025 in Zakarpattia.

  • Meeting on economic issues President of Russia 23 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a meeting on economic issues.

  • Foreign Ministry statement on Western countries' unlawful actions in the world's oceans Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 23 Mar 2026

  • The Mess With Max, Telegram, And More: Russians' Patience Tested By Online Troubles RFE/RL 23 Mar 2026 -- WhatsApp is throttled. Telegram is about to be blocked. Soldiers are angry. Max, a state-backed messenger app, is being forced on everyone. Commanders warn Max is unsafe. Average Russians are trying to avoid it.

  • Wang Yi Meets with UK Prime Minister's National Security Adviser Jonathan Powell PRC MofA 23 Mar 2026
  • China's Smart Dragon-3 rocket launches satellites from sea Global Times 23 Mar 2026 -- A Smart Dragon-3 rocket lifted off on Sunday in east China's Shandong Province, sending the satellite group CentiSpace 02 into the planned orbit.

  • MAC head refuses to answer questions from China-born TPP lawmaker CNA 23 Mar 2026 -- Taiwan's top China affairs official on Monday refused to answer questions from China-born Taiwan People's Party (TPP) Legislator Li Chen-hsiu (李貞秀) during a legislative committee session, saying it would be "illegal" to answer someone not legally qualified to hold office.
  • Fujian Coast Guard conducts routine law-enforcement patrols in the waters near Kinmen Global Times 23 Mar 2026 -- Zhu Anqing, spokesperson of China Coast Guard (CCG) Donghai Branch, stated on Monday that Fujian Coast Guard conducted routine law-enforcement patrols in the waters near Kinmen, according to a statement released by CCG on its official WeChat account.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 23 Mar 2026

  • Proposal for Electing Comrade Kim Jong Un as President of State Affairs of DPRK KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea conducted the important and responsible work for electing the head of state on March 22.
  • Opening Speech at First Session of 15th SPA KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- Deputy Ko Kil Son, secretary general of the Standing Committee of the Supreme People's Assembly, made an opening speech at the First Session of the 15th SPA.
  • Standing Committee of SPA Elected at First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea elected the Standing Committee of the SPA as follows:
  • Director of SPPO Appointed, President of SC Elected at First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea appointed the director of the Supreme Public Prosecutors Office and elected the president of the Supreme Court as follows:
  • Cabinet Members Appointed at First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea appointed the members of the Cabinet as follows:
  • SPA Sectional Committees Elected at First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the DPRK elected the Legislation Committee, the Budget Committee and the Foreign Affairs Committee, SPA sectional committees, as follows:
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Reelected as President of State Affairs of DPRK KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The election of the president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea representing the state and its people is the most important state affairs that the supreme power body determines on behalf of the unanimous will of all the Korean people for the destiny and future of tens of millions of people, the eternal dignity and mightiness of the DPRK and for leading the great pioneering period of national rejuvenation unprecedented in the history of its founding to an everlasting heyday.
  • State Affairs Commission Elected at First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea elected the State Affairs Commission as follows:
  • First-day Sitting of First Session of 15th SPA of DPRK Held KCNA 23 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea was opened in the capital city of Pyongyang at a historic time when all the people across the country have courageously turned out in the patriotic struggle to usher in a new heyday of steady leap forward and development of our great state, true to the decisions of the Ninth Congress of the Workers' Party of Korea.

  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: SPACE DOCKING TECHNOLOGY India PIB 23 Mar 2026 -- ISRO is studying two orbital docking experiments viz

  • Joint Statement on Energy Security Prime Minister of Australia 23 Mar 2026
  • Opening remarks meeting with Executive Director of the International Energy Agency Prime Minister of Australia 23 Mar 2026

  • More Cubans Are Choosing to Stay in Latin America, New IOM Data Shows IOM 23 Mar 2026 -- A new report from the International Organization for Migration's (IOM) Displacement Tracking Matrix (DTM) shows shifting migration patterns among Cubans across Latin America, with more people not only moving through the region but also choosing to remain there.

  • UN weather agency warns of record 'climate imbalance' as planetary warming accelerates UN News 23 Mar 2026 -- All-time high greenhouse gas concentrations in Earth's atmosphere continue to drive heat records on land and sea, with long-lasting consequences for humanity, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) warned on Monday.
